title: We Have Been SWAYED: RHOA Recap with Dustin, Claudia & Jazmine
summary: RHOA fans, weвҖҷre here with an epic recap from Carlos King, Dustin Ross, Claudia Jordan, and Jazmine Henley Brown! We loved this episode and dare we say it was even better than the premiere?! Our girl Claudia was featured, and sheвҖҷs here to give her behind the scenes perspective. And does Porsha have a new BAE?! Of course, we also get into the latest custody and house updates involving Drew Sidora and Ralph Pittman. Reigndrops, this is a GOOD one! See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: New Parents | "The Ride" with Director Jon Huertas (509)
summary: On todayвҖҷs episode of That Was Us, weвҖҷre breaking down This Is Us Season 5, Episode 9: вҖңThe Ride.вҖқ As new parents bring their babies home, the episode explores the fears, hopes, and expectations that come with the start of a new chapter for every Pearson family. Across timelines, itвҖҷs a reminder that the ride home is where everything begins. In this episode, the hosts chat about: * Sharing their own stressful ride-home and early parenting experiences * The emotional chaos of bringing newborns home across timelines * Jack and RebeccaвҖҷs trip home with the triplets * JackвҖҷs anxiety about becoming his father, including the gas station confrontation and his admitted drink * Kevin and Madison navigating paparazzi and the pressure of new parenthood * KevinвҖҷs dream conversation with Jack and his spur-of-the-moment proposal * Kate and Toby bringing home Hailey and Ellie backing out of the open adoption arrangement * Toby revealing that heвҖҷs been fired * Future Deja arriving at RandallвҖҷs home pregnant and the showвҖҷs seamless casting continuity Plus, Jon Huertas joins the podcast to talk about: * Directing вҖңThe RideвҖқ during COVID * His transition from actor to director * What he finds most fulfilling about directing * The preparation that set him up for success behind the camera * Fun memories directing Mandy Moore, Chris Sullivan, and Sterling K. title: Josh Einsohn and Us | Casting Director
summary: This week on That Was Us, weвҖҷre joined by one of the most pivotal people behind This Is Us: casting director Josh Einsohn. Josh reflects on 25 years in casting and shares how he got his start in production, what led him to feel passionate about casting, and how This Is Us ultimately changed his life forever. In this episode, Josh chats about: * His 25-year journey in casting and how he got his start * The role Tommy Lee Jones played early in his career * Working on The Polar Express and The West Wing * Partnering with Tiffany Little Canfield to cast This Is Us * Bringing Jon Huertas onto the show * The pressure of casting multiple ages of the same characters * The urgent recasting of the teen Big Three * How auditions changed after COVID * Casting Griffin Dunne as Nicky * Casting Jennifer Morrison as Cassidy * His advice for actors navigating the industry * How This Is Us influenced his decision to adopt his daughter, Molly" That Was Us is produced by Rabbit Grin Productions. title: That Was Us LIVE! At The Wiltern in Los Angeles
summary: This week on That Was Us, weвҖҷre bringing you a very special episode recorded live at the historic Wiltern Theater in Los Angeles. In front of an incredible audience of This Is Us fans! It was an unforgettable night of conversation and live music with Sterling K. Brown, Mandy Moore, and Chris Sullivan, as we celebrated one of the most powerful elements of This Is Us: the music. We talked about the songs that shaped some of the showвҖҷs most emotional moments, and were joined by the showвҖҷs composer, Siddhartha Khosla, as well as the creator of the show, Dan Fogelman, to hear how the score came to life. We also played a live round of Hot Takes with the audience and, of course, the night featured incredible live performances from guests like Taylor Goldsmith, Hannah Zeile, Blake Stadnik, and more, making it a truly special celebration of the music and community that made This Is UsвҖҰand That Was UsвҖҰso meaningful. Creator of Strip Law on Netflix, Cullen Crawford, joins Matt & Vince to talk about Mad Men season four episode five, вҖңThe Chrysanthemum and the Sword.вҖқ Mad Men is, if nothing else, a television show, which means there has to be at least one episode in which important Japanese businessmen need to be impressed. HondaвҖҷs motorcycle men are shopping for a new ad agency and if you thought Roger was racist before, youвҖҷve never seen him interact with people from the country whose servicemen killed his buddies in Dubya Dubya II. He does his best to verbally A-bomb the deal, but Don hatches a plan to impress Honda while dunking on Ted Chaough. You donвҖҷt get to be that good at your job without also neglecting your kids, so on his one night with Sally and Bobby, he leaves them with his neighbor Phoebe so he can go on a date. While heвҖҷs gone, Sally cuts her own hair. Betty is pissed because she lacks the foresight to understand her new do would be very chic in Silver Lake today.В  What should we order at Benihana? title: Corrie's Most Memorable Funerals
summary: In this week's bonus podcast, we chat about one of Corrie's most enduring traditions вҖ“ the Weatherfield funeral. There've been far too many over the years for us to cover them all, so here weвҖҷre looking back at some of the most memorable, important and notorious send-offs that Coronation Street has ever given its residents. From the more solemn, atmospheric funerals of the showвҖҷs early days to the more dramatic services where secrets spill out and punches are thrown, we explore just how the cobbles have said goodbye to their own across the decades. And of course, because this is Corrie, there's also time to reminisce about some of the more awkward and unexpectedly funny funeral moments too!

title: TJG: Summer House S10 E6!

title: S5E6 вҖ“ 'Far Away Places'
summary: вҖңFar Away PlacesвҖқ is one of those Mad Men episodes where the show quietly changes shape. This week on Mad Men Men, we dig into that Season 5 standout. First, the episode fractures a single day into three interlocking stories. Then, it follows Peggy, Roger, and Don as each tries to escape a life that feels unbearable.  Meanwhile, Peggy chases professional authority and confronts how lonely ambition can feel. Roger drops acid and stumbles into one of the seriesвҖҷ clearest emotional revelations. At the same time, Don takes a spontaneous trip with Megan. Yet again, he learns that fantasy is easy to build and nearly impossible to inhabit.  Along the way, we explore why this might be Mad Men at its most novelistic. We also talk about why the experimentation never feels gimmicky. Instead, it deepens everything. And likewise, the episodeвҖҷs mirrors, doubling, and displacement turn three plots into variations on one theme.  ThereвҖҷs also room for what our show does best. Yes, we riff on orange sherbet, bad intros, and Howard JohnsonвҖҷs as a site of psychic collapse. And then we talk about the strange relief of realizing your life has become untenable.  If youвҖҷre new here, Mad Men Men recaps Mad Men through three perspectives. First, we have our first-time viewer. Then thereвҖҷs our first-time rewatcher. Finally, thereвҖҷs the guy who has to podcast about everything instead of going to therapy.  EXTRA CREDITS  Matthew Weiner createdВ Mad Men, which aired on AMC from 2007 to 2015. The show stars Jon Hamm, January Jones, Elisabeth Moss, Vincent Kartheiser, Christina Hendricks, John Slattery, Robert Morse, and many more. Our intro music is вҖңMad Men MenвҖқ by Tom Davidson, which is an original remix of the showвҖҷs opening theme вҖңA Beautiful MineвҖқ by RJD2. Podcast illustration is by Jon Negroni. Our podcast hosts include Jon NegroniВ (Podcast Editor ofВ InBetweenDrafts),В Will AshtonВ (cohost of theВ CinemaholicsВ podcast), andВ Michael OverhulseВ (a guy whoвҖҷs addicted to working at startups).  WeвҖҷll be back soon to discuss Season 5 Episode 7, titled вҖңAt the Codfish Ball.вҖқ Subscribe to Mad Men MenВ onВ Apple Podcasts,В Spotify,В Google Podcasts, or wherever else podcasts are, ahem, advertised. summary: Tara Lipinski and Johnny Weir Traitors S4 Deep Dive Tara Lipinski and Johnny Weir join Rob Cesternino and Pooya for a deep-dive conversation on The Traitors: Season 4. The two Olympic figure skating analysts swap the ice for the castle, exploring how their friendship shapes their game. Both as an asset and a target. With the pressures of reality TV clashing against their competitive instincts, the pair reveal what it takes to survive late-game paranoia and shifting alliances. Tara and Johnny open up about their unique experience balancing Olympic broadcasting in Milan with the real-time drama of The Traitors. They recall pivotal votes and the daily anxiety of hiding (and then exposing) their friendship. The discussion highlights key turning points, from decisions around loyalty and integrity to their approach after finally playing openly as a duo. Stories unfold about close calls, intense roundtables, and the struggle to sway vote outcomes as the endgame narrows.    Dive into the moment Tara and Johnny disclose their friendship, changing their approach and opening new gameplay opportunities    Explore MauraвҖҷs crucial voting decisions, the efforts Tara and Johnny make to win her over, and the emotional fallout from trusting alliances that donвҖҷt hold    Get a behind-the-scenes look at the numbers game. How Johnny keeps a constant count to maintain roundtable control, while Tara relies on gut instincts to spot traitors    Relive the shock of CandaceвҖҷs reveal as a traitor and how that vote shapes Tara and JohnnyвҖҷs strategy moving forward    Hear candid stories about balancing fun, emotional stress, and real-life friendships behind castle walls How do you stick together when the whole game is built on suspicion and betrayal? Can trust and teamwork outplay tight alliances and shifting targets? Listen as Tara and Johnny break down the late-game pressure, share hidden highlights, and reflect on the line between friendship and strategy in The Traitors. DonвҖҷt miss this revealing conversation for fans of strategy, social dynamics, and the unpredictable bonds built during a reality competition. 00:00 Tara Lipinski joins the podcast13:11 Forming Early Game Alliances17:37 Explosive Banquet and Suspicion Peaks23:38 TaraвҖҷs Gut Targets Rob34:39 Trust, Codes, and Vote Strategy41:35 Shock Over CandaceвҖҷs Traitor Reveal47:07 Solving the Game, a Comedic Finale50:04 Prep for The Traitors Detailed56:44 Behind-the-Scenes Gameplay Protection01:03:15 Aftermath: Separation and Emotional Toll01:08:30 Minimizing Suspicion, Near Victory01:13:38 Potential Amazing Race Future Never miss all our Traitors US recaps plus recaps from other versions around the world! title: рҹ—һпёҸ Bravo Weekly News: Sunday, March 8, 2026
podcast_name: The Bravo Papers: Weekly News & Commentary with Bravo & Botox
summary: The rumours have been flying this week that Amanda Batula and West Wilson are hooking up! Deux Moi reports that they've been seen all over NYC making out, but is it true? I've got everything we know so far. In RHOP news, Ashley Darby reveals what was on that bleeped out recording that we didn't hear on the third part of the RHOP Reunion, and we have some updates on the trial for Wendy and Eddie Osefo (and it's much sooner than you might think). title: Conversation Street Episode 722
summary: This podcast covers the episodes of Coronation Street shown between the 2nd and the 5th March (Episodes #11,805 - 11,808). We're feeling pretty smug this week as one of our predictions for 2026 has already come true: the arrival of Mandy's baby at the Connor-Swain residence! And colour us surprised: we actually really enjoyed the scenes too! Could Carla and Lisa actually be in for that rare thing in soaps - a bit of happiness? (Or at least before that dead body puts a dampener on their wedding day...) Bad news for fans of Jim McDonald, though, the big man himself has said cheerio now for the final time and gone to that big pub in the sky, so he has - and we seem tantalisingly close to discovering whether that theorised link between him and Maggie is true too... Also this week, baby Harper goes under the knife, Lou gets beaten up in prison and Todd struggles to buy Theo a birthday present. Another news-light week this week, so we skip over The Kabin segment and finish things off with more of your listener feedback. Street Talk - 00:13:15 Feedback - 02:22:31

title: S2E64-Severed ORIGINS-Eternal Sunshine-PT05
summary: THIS IS THE 'SEVERED: ORIGINS' BREAKDOWN OF 'ETERNAL SUNSHINE' - PT05 Welcome back for Part Five of this Ultimate breakdown of "Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind." If you've come this far, there's not a lot I can say. It's time to delve into Joel's humiliations. This is the cringy stuff Charlie Kaufman really likes to write about. It might leave you feeling just a bit sick to your stomach...that's okay, it's the CK way. Mrs. Howard makes a visit! Mary starts to kiss Howard right in front of the big window that's all lit up all of a sudden in Joel's apartment. They start this at the exact same moment as Mrs. Mierzwiak comes rolling up in her Chevy Sedan. Hollis unloads on Howard plus she spills the beans about Mary. At least she causes Mary to ask some questions that Howard finds hard to answer. Deceiving Mary will prove to be a very bad idea.В  This ORIGINS entry is a trippy 2004 title from French Director Michel Gondry. Both Dan Erickson and Ben Stiller list it as an inspiration. It stars Kate Winslet and Jim Carrey. This is considered one of the best things Jim Carrey has ever done. Get ready for Part FIVE of this enormous movie breakdown. ******* Huge thanks to Adam Scott, star of 'Severance' and host of the Severance Podcast for recording a custom intro for "Severed." Make sure to check out 'The Severance Podcast w/Ben Stiller & Adam Scott" wherever you found this one! A big 'thank you' to friend of the pod Kier Eagan, er I mean Marc Geller! Marc both sat for an interview (make sure to check it out) AND recorded some great bumpers as Kier himself. Follow Marc on Instagram @geller_marc. BIG Thanks to Friend of the Podcast Hayley Erickson-Goelzer. She is Sister to creator Dan Erickson and also a listener. It took getting approval from Dan's Press Person, but thanks to her follow-through we've got a custom bumper from Hayley Erickson-Goelzer!! Yes, that is really and truly Primary Director and Executive Producer Ben Stiller doing a custom bumper for 'Severed.' He actually did this bumper for the show while we were recording an interview for the Official Ben & Adam Podcast. Thank you, Ben for the Bumper and for bringing this incredible show to the screen!! summary: Lisa faces the fallout of her lies. Forrest clashes with Molly over his engagement. Aviva goes to the source for answers about Stig's infidelity. Elise flies to Australia to meet Joshua for the first time. Rick tries to regain Trish's trust. --- Vanity Farah is a fun and femme coded pop culture recap podcast with Chris Farah, an actress, writer, comedian who is obsessed with pointing out the aesthetic choices and beauty trends she sees in reality shows like 90 Day FiancГ© and Love is Blind. title: рҹ—һпёҸ Bravo Weekly News: Sunday, March 1, 2026
podcast_name: The Bravo Papers: Weekly News & Commentary with Bravo & Botox
summary: On today's episode, we start some heartbreaking news about the passing of Mary Cosby's son, Robert Jr. We send all our thoughts, prayers and support to Mary Cosby during this devastating time. Next, I get into some Summer House news, as questions swirl on social media about Amanda Batula's possible new relationship. Is she soft-launching her new guy? Plus, did Lindsay Hubbard and Ben Waddell hook up? The RHOA Season 17 trailer has dropped and K. Michelle and Drew Sidora are already beefing online (in the funniest way possible). Plus, guess who's out of jail early?! In RHOBH/Traitors US news, Lisa Rinna and Andy Cohen have "squashed their beef" and she reveals her unfiltered thoughts about her former cast mates in her new book along with some shocking news from the Trailers US premiere party! title: Conversation Street Episode 721
summary: On our latest podcast, we chat about the episodes of Coronation Street shown between the 23rd and the 27th February (Episodes #11,801 - 11,805). This week on the cobbles, the Mal storyline kicked up a gear as BernieвҖҷs unwanted admirer crossed the line from irritating nuisance to full-on physcho stalker вҖ“ only to end up in intensive care after a brutal attack by an unknown assailant. In true Weatherfield fashion, Bernie is wrongfully arrested, though it's soon revealed that that it was Kit who bonked him on the head. With a tenacious colleague sniffing around the case, could DC GreenвҖҷs career now be hanging in the balance? Elsewhere on the street, a little more of Jodie's mysterious past comes to light when a young girl who she seemingly nannied for once show up to meet her - plus we see another item added to her secret box of trinkets... Meanwhile, Steve learns that his dad has been taken into hospital, George worries that a string of blunders at the undertakerвҖҷs could damage the businessвҖҷs reputation, and the spark between Ollie and Lauren is swiftly extinguished following an intervention from Maggie. Up next on the podcast, it's The Kabin, and we weigh in on some candid comments from Maureen Lipman and Sally Ann Matthews about the state of the show at the moment, all of which is pretty timely in the wake of Iain MacLeod's interview last week! We round off the show with more of your feedback. Street Talk - 00:13:44 The Kabin - 02:38:44 Feedback - 03:00:46

title: Sex and the City S4 E16 "Ring a Ding Ding"
summary: This may be one of the most relatable episdes ever for any diva who hasnt really ever thrived at budgeting or money management. Carrie is left scrambling to come up with $30k for a down payment and she has about ten minutes to do so (thanks so much, Aiden!). Samantha perfectly manipultes an "I Love You" out of Richard, which we already know is beginning the snowball into Hell. podcast_name: Pod Yourself A Gun - A Rewatch Podcast
summary: TheyвҖҷre not Homosexuals, TheyвҖҷre Divorced Great news, itвҖҷs a Don in California episode of Mad Men and a banger episode of the pod. Head of video at Yahoo Sports and OG friend of the Frot, Matt Ufford, joins Matt and Vince to talk about season four episode three, вҖңThe Good News.вҖқ ItвҖҷs too bad Anna Draper has to die. Don really couldnвҖҷt ask for a cooler, chiller girl to be the wife of the guy whose identity he stole. All she wants from him is to hang out, smoke grass, nail her teenage niece, and keep paying her mortgage. ItвҖҷs a sweet deal for everyone involved, but all sweet deals must come to end. AnnaвҖҷs got (leg?) cancer. SheвҖҷs gonna die, and Don is sad about it.В  The good news for you the viewer is that Mad Men is often funniest when the mad men are sad men. Back in New York, one of Mad MenвҖҷs saddest men, Lane Pryce, is feeling very divorced and ready to hang, so they catch a Godzilla flick, get crowd worked by Finn DeTrolio, and end the night with some sex workers. It sounds pathetic, but feeling so pathetic is what motivates a man to pretend his steak is a big olвҖҷ Texas belt buckle. What movie did you see when you got divorced? title: Traitors S4 Finale + Reunion Recap
summary: Traitors S4 Finale + Reunion Recap Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. Rob Cesternino and Pooya  recap the Traitors Season 4 finale and reunion, breaking down a decisive endgame that ends with Rob Roush securing a solo traitor victory. They analyze how the final Fire of Truth unfolded, why Maura never seriously questioned Rob despite mounting evidence, and how EricвҖҷs late attempt to flip the game came far too late to matter . The discussion focuses on RobвҖҷs endgame positioning, particularly his ability to maintain MauraвҖҷs loyalty while allowing her fixation on Eric to dictate the final vote. Rob and Pooya examine whether Rob would have accepted a shared win, concluding that circumstances (not premeditation) pushed him toward cutting Eric for a solo payout. They also credit RobвҖҷs helicopter mission time with Maura as a subtle but decisive moment that locked in her trust. Attention turns to Tara and Johnny, with the hosts agreeing that Tara ultimately had the correct read but lacked the numbers to act. They break down where the faithful path collapsed, including the timing of accusations and missed opportunities to remove Eric earlier. The reunion recap covers CandaceвҖҷs confrontation with Rob, with Rob and Pooya agreeing that her accusations lacked substantive evidence. They discuss the HousewivesвҖҷ reunion energy, Lisa RinnaвҖҷs standout presence, and the overall imbalance between strategic discussion and interpersonal conflict. The episode closes with a broader assessment of Rob RoushвҖҷs legacy. Rob and Pooya debate his place among the greatest traitors in franchise history, ultimately framing Season 4 as one of the strongest finales the series has produced, marked by clean execution, sustained deception, and a decisive traitor win. title: S2E63-Severed ORIGINS-Eternal Sunshine-PT04
summary: THIS IS THE 'SEVERED: ORIGINS' BREAKDOWN OF 'ETERNAL SUNSHINE' - PT04 Welcome back for Part Four of this ultimate breakdown of "Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind." If you've come this far, there's not a lot I can say. We are deeply into Joel's brain. Stan and Mary are naked at Joel's place and Weasel-Patrick is laying on the frozen Charles with a deeply manic Clementine. Howard's coming! Joel's antics, trying to avoid the wipe, have caused him to disappear from Stan's wipe maps. Dr. Howard Mierzwiak makes an appearance at Joel's place to get it back on track. This ORIGINS entry is a trippy 2004 title from French Director Michel Gondry. Both Dan Erickson and Ben Stiller list it as an inspiration. It stars Kate Winslet and Jim Carrey. This is considered one of the best things Jim Carrey has ever done. Get ready for Part FOUR of this enormous movie breakdown. ******* Huge thanks to Adam Scott, star of 'Severance' and host of the Severance Podcast for recording a custom intro for "Severed." Make sure to check out 'The Severance Podcast w/Ben Stiller & Adam Scott" wherever you found this one! A big 'thank you' to friend of the pod Kier Eagan, er I mean Marc Geller! Marc both sat for an interview (make sure to check it out) AND recorded some great bumpers as Kier himself. Follow Marc on Instagram @geller_marc. BIG Thanks to Friend of the Podcast Hayley Erickson-Goelzer. She is Sister to creator Dan Erickson and also a listener. It took getting approval from Dan's Press Person, but thanks to her follow-through we've got a custom bumper from Hayley Erickson-Goelzer!! Yes, that is really and truly Primary Director and Executive Producer Ben Stiller doing a custom bumper for 'Severed.' He actually did this bumper for the show while we were recording an interview for the Official Ben & Adam Podcast. Thank you, Ben for the Bumper and for bringing this incredible show to the screen!! title: TJG: Southern Charm S11 E13!
summary: WeвҖҷre still in Puerto Vallarta and Salley finally plants a kiss on Austen: in the bathroom, of all places. Austen canвҖҷt help but kiss and tell sharing it with Craig, who then tells Austen heвҖҷs made out with Charley. Austen quickly runs to tell Salley and she canвҖҷt help but think Charley is lying to her. Craigy takes the ride home and goes off on Salley, while Charley sits in the backseat horrified. title: Love Island All Stars S3 - Finale + Couple Predictions!
podcast_name: Proper Chat: A Love Island Podcast
summary: Welcome to Proper Chat: A Love Island Podcast! In this episode, Nicole is joined by co-hosts Langa, Brana, and Gabi to break down Love Island All Stars Season 3вҖҷs final week (Episodes 32вҖ“39) and the live finaleвҖ”full spoilers ahead. They recap the phone calls from home, the вҖңknowing me, knowing youвҖқ couples quiz, Whitney and YeamanвҖҷs date, and Sean and LucindaвҖҷs sudden boyfriend/girlfriend moment (plus Sean dropping an вҖңI love youвҖқ).  The hosts dive into the public Q&A vote (including the Scott/Leanne question that set off Hurricane Belle again), MayaвҖҷs on-air prompts, and the tension around SophieвҖҷs comments about BelleвҖҷs treatment of Lucinda. They discuss Sean and Lucinda saying they would dump Tommy and Jesse, HelenaвҖҷs take that Jesse was with Tommy for airtime, and whether Sean has any loyalty to Tommy. The episode also covers the abrupt dumpings (Tommy and Jesse; then Belle and Harrison after the ex-IslandersвҖҷ least-compatible vote), with returning Islanders stirring things upвҖ”Charlie calling Scott a snake, AJ citing LaurenвҖҷs podcast claims about Harrison, and Jack reviving the Pepper Mayo вҖңsugar daddyвҖқ rumors about Lucinda. In the finale recap, they touch on awkward moments with Curtis and Kira, Harrison still denying LaurenвҖҷs allegations, and reveal the final results: 5th Whitney & Yeaman, 4th Lucinda & Sean, 3rd Leanne & Scott, 2nd Millie & Zach, and winners Sammy & Kieran. They play a detailed predictions/tier game on how long each couple will last post-villa, then wrap with вҖңScoop on the Streets,вҖқ including the final vote percentages (Sammy/Kieran 35%, Millie/Zach 23%, Leanne/Scott 19%, Lucinda/Sean 12%, Whitney/Yeaman 9%) and news that Maya will return for the summer season. title: Court On Camera!
summary: We recently headed up north for another Corrie location hunt, ticking off several spots that were completely new to us. First up was The Saddleworth Hotel, seen earlier this year as the setting for DebbieвҖҷs ill-fated wedding, followed by a visit to BoltonвҖҷs Old MagistratesвҖҷ Court вҖ“ a familiar backdrop for some of Coronation StreetвҖҷs most explosive trials in recent years. While we were there, we also caught three former Coronation Street stars on stage at the Octagon Theatre, explored the latest additions to the MediaCity tour, and tracked down a couple of other locations that have popped up in smaller scenes over the years. title: The Begotten
summary: The Delta Flyers is hosted by Garrett Wang, Robert Duncan McNeill, Terry Farrell & Armin Shimerman. In each podcast release, they will recap and discuss an episode of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine. This weekвҖҷs episode, The Begotten, is hosted by Garrett Wang, Robert Duncan McNeill, and Armin Shimerman. The Begotten: Deep Space Nine celebrates the birth of the O'Brien baby, as Odo and Dr. Mora struggle to save the life of a Changeling infant. title: The Traitors S4 Finale Preview
summary: The Traitors S4 Finale Preview Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. The Traitors Season 4 finale is just around the corner, and Rob Cesternino, Pooya, and guest Mike Bloom dig into all the shifting alliances, metagame shake-ups, and Faithful-versus-Traitor strategies shaping this pivotal episode. With the game at a crossroads, the hosts unpack how the shifting dynamics among reality TV stars, non-gamers, and Housewives set the stage for an unexpected endgame. The discussion covers the subtle evolution of the cast, and how outsider groups reach the finale while Survivor and Big Brother veterans get picked off. Rob shares his perspective as a recent cast member, weighing in on how alliances like the "Dagger Six" and the coalition of more introverted players fly under the radar, building unique routes to the end. The group also explores how Rob Roush has navigated group loyalty, traitor recruitment, and critical late-game decisions without burning his key relationships. Highlights from their conversation include:    The metagameвҖҷs impact on targeting specific groups (gamers, Housewives, non-gamers) and how this shapes who reaches the finale    Rob RoushвҖҷs alliance maneuveringвҖ”balancing connections with both lower-profile coalitions and the larger "Dagger Six"    The strategy and risks behind traitor recruitment, including why Rob picks Eric over other potential options for a final stretch partnership    How Faithfuls should approach late-game votes when strong duos like Johnny and Tara could dominate the ending    A lively debate comparing Rob RoushвҖҷs gameplay to Cirie Fields, sizing up their disarming social strategies and influence in the castle Will RobвҖҷs partnership choices and social game secure him the win, or could Faithfuls splinter the duos before the fire of truth? Dive in as the hosts break down the possible paths to victory, weigh All-Star returnee picks, and tease what explosive reveals the reunion might hold. title: Traitors US S4 Week 7 Exit Interview w/ Banished Player
summary: Traitors US S4 Week 7 Exit Interview w/ Banished Player Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. В  Natalie Anderson sits down with Rob Cesternino and Pooya for an honest exit interview after her banishment from the latest Traitors roundtable. Natalie reflects on her time as the final gamer standing and the difficult decisions she faced in the castle. With emotions running high and trust broken, Natalie and the hosts dig into the social dynamics, alliances, and game-changing moments that shaped her exit. Natalie opens up about her strategies and regrets, revealing how alliances were lost and how she could have approached her last day differently. She explains the challenges of convincing others to vote against Rob and discusses the isolating tactics used by Johnny and Tara, which kept her cut off from key players like Mark, Maura, and Eric. The conversation turns to the mysterious dagger, the hesitance from others to target Rob, and how the perception of being a "gamer" created extra heat and suspicion for Natalie throughout her time in the castle. Key moments from the interview:    Natalie considers redirecting votes toward Rob after realizing Tara was not on her side.    JohnnyвҖҷs influence shapes TaraвҖҷs decision at the roundtable, fueling NatalieвҖҷs frustration.    The dagger controversy complicates trust and alliances, raising suspicions about RobвҖҷs motives.    Natalie's analysis of EricвҖҷs covert gameplay and why he remained under the radar.    Insight into the anti-gamer bias in the castle and how it affected strategic choices and banishments. NatalieвҖҷs deep dive into her Traders experience uncovers the mental toll of isolation, the intricacies of castle politics, and the fallout of not being able to unite fellow gamers. Will the lingering gamer bias shift in future episodes, or will strategic targeting continue to dominate the gameвҖҷs endgame? title: 90 Day FiancГ©: Before the 90 Days Season 8 Episode вҖңUse Caution When Opening Overhead BinsвҖқ
summary: Lisa and Daniel visit an Igbo King. Emma's secret leaves her future with Ziad in jeopardy. Aviva presses Stig for the truth. Forrest plans a special day for Sheena. Elise prepares to meet her Australian boyfriend, Joshua. --- Vanity Farah is a fun and femme coded pop culture recap podcast with Chris Farah, an actress, writer, comedian who is obsessed with pointing out the aesthetic choices and beauty trends she sees in reality shows like 90 Day FiancГ© and Love is Blind. We deeply and sassily examine the choices that reality subjects make in the pursuit of love, and relate hard to the humanity on display, from questionable eyebrow shapes to the profound loneliness that plagues us all. If you want a smart yet silly friend to talk about dumb, escapist things with, subscribe, follow, and give 5 stars. title: Sister Wives 5.6 - Confronting Failure
summary: Christine tries to take her real estate license exam again, but she's struggling with self-confidence. The Brown family launches My Sister Wife's Closet, but why isn't anyone buying anything? Identity issues abounds as Kody and Christine in particular reveal a lot about themselves in this episode. title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms S1:E6 "The Morrow"
podcast_name: Streaming Things - A "The Last of Us" Podcast
summary: The first season of A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms comes to a thoughtful close. In this episode of Streaming Things, Kit, Andy, and Steve break down the season finale, вҖңThe Morrow.вҖқ In the aftermath of tragedy at Ashford, the weight of recent events hangs heavy over the tourney grounds. As the realm mourns a great loss, Dunk reflects on everything heвҖҷs endured - and faces a defining choice about his future, including whether Egg will remain by his side as his squire. title: Conversation Street Episode 720
summary: On our latest episode of the podcast, we chat about the goings on in Weatherfield between the 16th and the 20th February (Episodes #11,795 - 11,800). This week saw the much anticipated flash-forward episode of Corrie, which gave us a sneak peek into the future and Betsy's discovery of a dead body. Exactly who is the victim, though, and who could be responsible for causing their untimely demise? Also this week, Carl reveals to Ronnie the truth about his newly discovered relationship with Debbie, and we learn about his plans to burn down the hotel for the insurance money too - will Debbie realise what heвҖҷs really capable of before itвҖҷs too late? Elsewhere on the cobbles, Sam turns amateur detective in a bid to catch Megan and Will out, Lily starts her paper round at The Kabin, and Maggie grows uneasy over BenвҖҷs budding friendship with Steve. Could we be about to learn exactly what she found in KenвҖҷs cupboard? After Street Talk, we take quite a lengthy visit to The Kabin as we've got a fair bit to say about some recent news items. First, we react to the news that The British Soap Awards have once again been cancelled, and that's followed by our thoughts on Iain Macleod's recent appearance on the This Is Media City podcast, in which he previews some upcoming production changes to the show that it seems a lot of fans really aren't happy about... We round off the podcast with more of. Your fabulous feedback, including some initial reactions to this week's flash-forward episode. Street Talk - 00:09:39 The Kabin - 02:37:55 Feedback - 03:38:03

title: [MAD MEN] 402: Christmas Comes But Once a Year, with Emily Fleming
podcast_name: Pod Yourself A Gun - A Rewatch Podcast
summary: Christmas Lums Butt Once a Year It might be a balmy 82 degrees here in Los Angeles, but this weekвҖҷs episode is feeling a bit like Christmas. Our gift to you, co-host of the Free With Ads podcast, Emily Fleming, joins Matt and Vince to talk about Mad Men season four episode two, вҖңChristmas Comes But Once a Year.вҖқ Sterling Cooper Draper Pryce is throwing a Christmas party! Why? Is it because they love and respect our lord and savior Jesus Christ? They want their employees to feel appreciated? They just love the Christmas spirit? No! ItвҖҷs because one of the worst guys in the world is stopping by, and they need his money. Sort of like how you put on a sweater and see your Dad every December. Lee Garner Jr. is your dad in this analogy (cuz theyвҖҷre both closeted). Freddy Rumsen is back with dry pants and a new account, weird little GlenвҖҷs back with a lanyard for Sally, and regretfully, Don and his secretary are back in the office the day after he clumsily introduces her to his rye-pickled member. DonвҖҷt worry though, he gives her cash. That should make her feel good and not at all like a sex worker. What type of listener are you? One of the cool ones or one of the handsome ones? title: Traitors S4 Ep 10 Recap
summary: Traitors S4 Ep 10 Recap Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. Today, Rob Cesternino and Pooya discuss The Traitors US S4 Episode 10. Rob and Pooya recap the penultimate episode of The Traitors Season 4, breaking down a pivotal and emotional round table that ends with NatalieвҖҷs banishment. They analyze how Natalie finally pieced together RobвҖҷs position in the game, why her logic was correct, and how hesitation and numbers ultimately sealed her fate. The episode focuses on the brief but important alliance between Natalie and Tara, examining how they connected the dots around protection, survival, and who benefited most from recent murders. Rob and Pooya explain why JohnnyвҖҷs reluctance to commit fully to the plan proved decisive, leaving Natalie without the votes needed to force a tie or push Rob into real danger. They also break down the impact of RobвҖҷs dagger advantage, discussing how its presence discouraged risk-taking and narrowed the faithfulsвҖҷ viable paths forward. The hosts debate whether the faithful missed a critical opportunity by failing to force a split vote, and how MarkвҖҷs uncertainty and MauraвҖҷs loyalty kept Rob insulated. Rob and Pooya analyze the mission, EricвҖҷs shield win, and RobвҖҷs visible frustration after being eliminated early, questioning whether it revealed pressure or was simply situational intensity. They also discuss the strategic decision to murder Kristen instead of Natalie the night before, framing it as a clean setup for a successful banishment. The episode closes with a look ahead to the finale. Rob and Pooya outline the remaining power structure, assess the danger posed by the Tara/Johnny duo, and debate whether the traitors should target Mark next. With Natalie gone and the faithful divided, they frame the endgame as heavily favoring Rob, raising the question of whether he will share the prize or take it alone. title: S2E62-Severed ORIGINS-Eternal Sunshine-PT03
summary: THIS IS THE 'SEVERED: ORIGINS' BREAKDOWN OF 'ETERNAL SUNSHINE' - PT03 Welcome back for Part Three of this ultimate breakdown of "Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind." Now we've got Clem and PATRICK headed for the frozen Charles. Stan and Mary are partying over Joel's unconscious body as the mind wipe continues. Meanwhile, in his shorted out brain, Joel is trying to figure out how to hold onto at least a little bit of the memory of Clementine. You thought it was weird?? You ain't seen nothin' yet!! It's going to get REALLY weird this time out!В  This ORIGINS entry is a trippy 2004 title from French Director Michel Gondry. Both Dan Erickson and Ben Stiller list it as an inspiration. It stars Kate Winslet and Jim Carrey. This is considered one of the best things Jim Carrey has ever done. Get ready for Part THREE of this enormous movie breakdown. ******* Huge thanks to Adam Scott, star of 'Severance' and host of the Severance Podcast for recording a custom intro for "Severed." Make sure to check out 'The Severance Podcast w/Ben Stiller & Adam Scott" wherever you found this one! A big 'thank you' to friend of the pod Kier Eagan, er I mean Marc Geller! Marc both sat for an interview (make sure to check it out) AND recorded some great bumpers as Kier himself. Follow Marc on Instagram @geller_marc. BIG Thanks to Friend of the Podcast Hayley Erickson-Goelzer. She is Sister to creator Dan Erickson and also a listener. It took getting approval from Dan's Press Person, but thanks to her follow-through we've got a custom bumper from Hayley Erickson-Goelzer!! Yes, that is really and truly Primary Director and Executive Producer Ben Stiller doing a custom bumper for 'Severed.' He actually did this bumper for the show while we were recording an interview for the Official Ben & Adam Podcast. Thank you, Ben for the Bumper and for bringing this incredible show to the screen!! Support the Severed Podcast on Patreon! (Click here) APPLE PODCAST LISTENERS: If you are enjoying "Severed: The Ultimate 'Severance' Podcast" please make sure to leave a 5-star rating (and, if you want, a review telling others to give it a try). Higher rated podcasts get better placement in suggestion lists. It helps more "Severance" fans find the show. Thanks!!! (Unfortunately, I can't respond to any questions or comments made in Apple Podcast Reviews. Send those to: SeveredPod@gmail.com) Season 2 of "Severance" kicked off 1/17/2025 and ran through 3/20/2025.В  The Second Season of the "Severed" Podcast Rewatch Episodes kicked off on April 24th, 2025. title: Marciano vs. Demi: MomTokвҖҷs Legal Era Begins (Part 2)
summary: Trigger Warning: Discussions of sexual assault.В В  Legal Team,В weвҖҷreВ back in Salt Lake City and this weekВ weвҖҷreВ zooming out to look at the bigger legal picture in MarcianoвҖҷs lawsuit against Demi from The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives. After breaking down the drama, text messages, and on-screen fallout,В weвҖҷreВ now focusing on whatВ actually hasВ to be proven in court. From defamation standards to public figure hurdles, we unpack how reality TV narratives collide with real world legal thresholds and why this case may come downВ toВ much more than what aired on camera.В  WhatвҖҷsВ on the Docket? The specific claims Marciano filed against Demi and the production companyВ Whether calling someone a sexual predator qualifies as defamation per seВ What actual malice means and how public figure status raises the barВ UtahвҖҷs definitions of sexual assault versus sexual battery and why that distinction mattersВ The role an Anti SLAPP motion could play in shutting the case down earlyВ Why credibility not just contracts or clips may ultimately decide this lawsuitВ  Access additional content and our Patreon here: вҒ https://zez.am/thebravodocketвҒ В В  The Bravo Docket podcast, the statements we make whether in our own media or elsewhere, and any content we post are for entertainment purposes only and do not provide legal advice. title: Reality Check: Inside AmericaвҖҷs Next Top Model Documentary - Thoughts and Feels
summary: Hello Bravo Bosses and Happy Tuesday! This episode is audio only but you can watch the audio episode on YouTube! **TRIGGER WARNING - Discussions of SA. Today we are talking about the new Netflix documentary Reality Check: Inside AmericaвҖҷs Next Top Model which dives deep into the glittering highs and controversial lows of one of reality TVвҖҷs most iconic franchises: AmericaвҖҷs Next Top Model. Hosted by pop culture analysts and former reality TV insiders, this documentary unpacks how a modelling competition created by and starring Tyra Banks became a global phenomenonвҖ”launching careers, redefining beauty standards, and shaping the early-2000s reality TV boom. From unforgettable photo shoots and dramatic panel eliminations to behind-the-scenes production secrets, we explore what really happened when the cameras stopped rolling. We revisit the showвҖҷs most talked-about moments, examine critiques that resurfaced in the social media era, and ask: Did the series empower aspiring modelsвҖ”or exploit them? Featuring commentary on industry impact, reality TV ethics, and the evolution of fashion culture, this documentary offers a thoughtful rewatch of a show that helped define a generation of television. title: RobвҖҷs Reactions to The Traitors Episode 9
summary: RobвҖҷs Reactions to The Traitors Episode 9 Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. Today, Rob Cesternino and PooyaВ break down the twists, strategies, and shifting alliances among the remaining castle players. As friendships are exposed and traitors quietly maneuver, Rob and Pooya revisit pivotal voting blocks, surprise shortlist decisions, and clever gameplay, offering keen analysis on the evolving landscape of this reality competition. The podcast explores the multifaceted vote that sends Stephen Colletti home, featuring a unique 5-2-2 split and sharp insight into how pairs like Tara and Johnny, and Mark and Kristen, drive the action. Rob and Pooya unpack the missionвҖҷs meta-strategy where traitors cleverly channel suspicion, only for the faithful to stumble, sparking confusion and intrigue. The episode also looks at the fresh challenge of the death shortlist, and how self-preservation changes voting behavior, especially for those marked for elimination. Listeners are treated to candid takes on the growing dynamics, including the faithfulвҖҷs infighting, KristenвҖҷs direct confrontation with Eric, and EricвҖҷs rookie struggles as a newly recruited traitor. The looming presence of Rob RauschвҖ”a master of subtle manipulationвҖ”and the comedic contrast with EricвҖҷs anxious gameplay spark fun debate. The hosts examine murder tactics after the roundtable, shifting suspicions, and what the faithful need to do to mount a comeback. Key points discussed:    The strategic vote split and why certain pairs target differing suspects    How traitors hide in plain sight and influence the roundtable    The groupвҖҷs approach to the murder shortlist and its impact on vote strategy    KristenвҖҷs kitchen confrontation with Eric and its potential fallout    Rob RauschвҖҷs tactical use of alliances, crediting others for his choices Can Tara and JohnnyвҖҷs newfound friendship help them survive, or will the traitors keep dictating the flow until the finale? Unravel the chess moves, personality clashes, and bold calls as the endgame approaches. title: Conversation Street Episode 719
summary: On our latest podcast, we discuss the episodes of Coronation Street shown between the 9th and the 13th February (Episodes #11,790 - 11,794) This week, we meet Jodie and Shona's dad, Doug, though he's confined to his bed and certainly not in a good state of mind - though he's still lucid enough to know his youngest daughter's a wrong 'un. We're loving the mystery around this family, and Jodie's curious box of knickknacks has us wondering all sorts about what she's been up to! Also this week, Sam gets in touch with the Weatherfield drugs wizard when he hears that Ritalin can be used as a study aid - but is this a little too out of character for him? Also this week, Theo returns from his mum's, Mal proves himself to a a definite stalkery weirdo, and Debbie is assigned a prison buddy - and it's В someone we were very happy to see again! As much as we wanted to carry on with the podcast after this and discuss the cancellation of the BSAs, unfortunately Michael wasn't feeling too well at all, so we called it a day after Street Talk - so you'll just have to wait a little longer to hear our thoughts on the British Soap Awards' cancellation!

title: To The Moon And Back
podcast_name: Albert Square: After Dark - An EastEnders Podcast
summary: This week, Alfie gets a shock when the cousin he thought long since dead returns to offer help on the Zoe/Anthony/Chrissie situation - but will Chrissie have any light to shade on what exactly happened that Christmas night? Meanwhile, Bea finds herself out of a job and a home, but is Honey about to regret ever laying eyes on her?  Elsewhere, there's more medical drama for Nugget which leaves Ravi wracked with guilt, but is Mark on to him? And how much longer can Ravi keep his dark secrets from his family?

title: рҹ—һпёҸ Bravo Weekly News: Saturday, Feb. 14, 2026 (PART 2)
podcast_name: The Bravo Papers: Weekly News & Commentary with Bravo & Botox
summary: On Part 2 of this week's episode, we dive into some RHOP news, as the ladies react to Karen Huger's one on one sit down with Andy Cohen. Angel Massi accuses Ashley Darby of uses coded language to paint her husband, Bobby, as angry. In RHOBH news, Amanda Frances is clapping back at her castmates and says that Boz must be commenting on her business because her own online business isn't doing well. Amanda also discusses the cult she was in and responds to rumours that she quit mid-season. title: Traitors US S4 Week 6 Exit Interview w/ Banished Player
summary: Traitors US S4 Week 6 Exit Interview w/ Banished Player Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. The We Know Traitors podcast welcomes Stephen Colletti, fresh from his recent banishment on The Traitors. Host Pooya sits down with Stephen to dig into the pivotal moments that led to his exit from the castleвҖ”including game twists, shifting alliances, and a few frustrating misunderstandings. Stephen sheds light on how one banquet, a roast mix-up, and some questionable gut instincts among the players turned his game upside down. Stephen breaks down how the infamous tarot card incident becomes his undoing and reflects on missed clues left by Candiace and the tangled alliances with Johnny and Tara. The conversation dives into StephenвҖҷs perspective on NatalieвҖҷs influence, unpacking how suspicion shifts and why clear explanations sometimes fall on deaf ears in a high-pressure game. Stephen also opens up about his relationships with allies like Mark and Kristen, the reality of navigating trust and strategy, and how entering the house with and without pre-existing bonds changes everything.    Stephen reveals frustration over being accused due to misunderstandings around the roast and the tarot cards    Analysis of CandiaceвҖҷs strategic vote and how Stephen and Johnny fail to connect the breadcrumbs    Insights into NatalieвҖҷs gut-driven play and how it drives suspicion despite weak evidence    Discussion of alliance dynamics, especially between Johnny and Tara, and how trust shapes gameplay decisions    Reflections on the emotional aftermath of reality TV, StephenвҖҷs lessons from Laguna Beach, and his openness to taking on the Traitor role in the future Did NatalieвҖҷs hunch really seal StephenвҖҷs fate, or were there bigger game forces at work in the castle? Explore how shifting alliances and gut instincts can flip the game, and decide for yourself if StephenвҖҷs strategies gave him the best shot. title: Traitors S4 Ep 9 Recap w/ Sandra Diaz Twine
summary: Traitors S4 Ep 9 Recap w/ Sandra Diaz Twine Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. Today, Pooya and special guest, Sandra Diaz Twine, discuss The Traitors US S4 Episode 9. With Rob at the Traitors Reunion, Pooya recaps The Traitors Season 4, Episode 9 alongside special guest Sandra Diaz-Twine. Together, they break down a deceptively dense episode that reshapes the endgame, beginning with EricвҖҷs decision to accept the traitor ultimatum and officially join Rob in the turret The discussion focuses on EricвҖҷs adjustment to life as a traitor, including his increased nervousness, shifting behavior, and how those changes begin to draw suspicion from Kristen. Sandra and Pooya debate whether EricвҖҷs visible discomfort could become a liability, or if RobвҖҷs protection and social dominance will be enough to keep him insulated. They analyze DorindaвҖҷs murder and why her removal made strategic sense, particularly given her connections and lack of heat. The pair also breaks down the music box challenge, explaining why the traitors deliberately fed the faithful misleading information and why they avoided being placed on the final shortlist. At the round table, attention turns to Johnny and Stephen, with Pooya and Sandra dissecting how circumstantial evidence, such as past advocacy from Candace and Lisa, continues to follow them despite both being faithful. They also discuss Natalie being labeled the most manipulative player, her visible frustration, and how that perception may put her in danger. The episode closes with a forward-looking assessment of the remaining murder options, weighing Natalie versus Kristen, and why RobвҖҷs position appears nearly untouchable regardless of the outcome. With only two episodes left, Pooya and Sandra frame Episode 9 as a critical setup chapter that solidifies RobвҖҷs control while narrowing the faithfulвҖҷs already-limited paths to victory. Eloise Bridgerton (Claudia Jessie, Vanity Fair, Line of Duty) grows increasingly determined to uncover the identity of Lady Whistledown, while Penelope Featherington (Nicola Coughlan, Derry Girls, Barbie) and Colin Bridgerton (Luke Newton, The Lodge) navigate shifting romantic expectations. title: S2E61-Severed ORIGINS-Eternal Sunshine-PT02
summary: THIS IS THE 'SEVERED: ORIGINS' BREAKDOWN OF 'ETERNAL SUNSHINE' - PT02 Welcome back for Part Two of this ultimate breakdown of "Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind." We're headed to the Charles River for a 'Honeymoon on Ice.' Joel and Clementine, against all odds, are once again an item. We're also going to get to the offices of Lacuna, Inc...the mind wipe guys!В  This ORIGINS entry is a trippy 2004 title from French Director Michel Gondry. Both Dan Erickson and Ben Stiller list it as an inspiration. It stars Kate Winslet and Jim Carrey. This is considered one of the best things Jim Carrey has ever done. Get ready for Part TWO of this enormous movie breakdown. ******* Huge thanks to Adam Scott, star of 'Severance' and host of the Severance Podcast for recording a custom intro for "Severed." Make sure to check out 'The Severance Podcast w/Ben Stiller & Adam Scott" wherever you found this one! A big 'thank you' to friend of the pod Kier Eagan, er I mean Marc Geller! Marc both sat for an interview (make sure to check it out) AND recorded some great bumpers as Kier himself. Follow Marc on Instagram @geller_marc. BIG Thanks to Friend of the Podcast Hayley Erickson-Goelzer. She is Sister to creator Dan Erickson and also a listener. It took getting approval from Dan's Press Person, but thanks to her follow-through we've got a custom bumper from Hayley Erickson-Goelzer!! Yes, that is really and truly Primary Director and Executive Producer Ben Stiller doing a custom bumper for 'Severed.' He actually did this bumper for the show while we were recording an interview for the Official Ben & Adam Podcast. Thank you, Ben for the Bumper and for bringing this incredible show to the screen!! Support the Severed Podcast on Patreon! Higher rated podcasts get better placement in suggestion lists. It helps more "Severance" fans find the show. Thanks!!! (Unfortunately, I can't respond to any questions or comments made in Apple Podcast Reviews. Send those to: SeveredPod@gmail.com) Season 2 of "Severance" kicked off 1/17/2025 and ran through 3/20/2025.В  The Second Season of the "Severed" Podcast Rewatch Episodes kicked off on April 24th, 2025. title: INTERVIEW WITH THE VAMPIRE 1x4 REVIEW вҖ“ CLAUDIA'S TURN SURPRISED US! -FIRST TIME WATCHING
summary: CLAUDIA TAKES CENTER STAGE!! Interview With The Vampire Full Reaction Watch Along: В В /В thereelrejectsВ В    LIQUID IV: Visit http://www.liquidiv.com & use Promo Code: REJECTS   Gift Someone (Or Yourself) An RR Tee!  https://shorturl.at/hekk2   Interview with the Vampire Ep 1 REACTION: В В В вҖўВ INTERVIEWВ WITHВ THEВ VAMPIREВ S1В EPISODEВ 1В RE...В В    Interview with the Vampire Ep 2 REACTION: В В В вҖўВ INTERVIEWВ WITHВ THEВ VAMPIREВ S1В EPISODEВ 2В RE...В В    Interview with the Vampire (1994) Movie Reaction: В В В вҖўВ INTERVIEWВ WITHВ THEВ VAMPIREВ (1994)В MOVIEВ RE...В В  Greg & Roxy CONTINUE their Interview with the Vampire Reaction, Recap, Analysis, Breakdown, Commentary, & Spoiler Review!! Greg Alba and Roxy Striar return with their reaction and review of Season 1, Episode 4 of AMCвҖҷs Interview with the Vampire, titled вҖңThe Ruthless Pursuit of Blood with All a ChildвҖҷs Demanding.вҖқ Based on Anne RiceвҖҷs iconic novel, this episode marks a major turning point for the series as the introduction of Claudia permanently reshapes the twisted bond between Louis and Lestat. In this haunting chapter, Louis de Pointe du Lac (Jacob Anderson, Game of Thrones, Broadchurch) and Lestat de Lioncourt (Sam Reid, The Newsreader, Belle) grapple with the consequences of turning a plague-ravaged child into a vampire. Claudia (Bailey Bass, Avatar: The Way of Water, A House on the Bayou) quickly proves to be far more dangerous and emotionally volatile than either of them anticipated, unleashing a ruthless hunger that mirrors and magnifies LestatвҖҷs cruelty. The episode delivers unforgettable moments of gothic horror and dark irony, from ClaudiaвҖҷs first kills to her chilling realization of her eternal imprisonment in a childвҖҷs body. As Louis begins to see Claudia as both daughter and reflection of his own damnation, tensions escalate inside the immortal family, setting the stage for betrayal, resentment, and long-brewing revenge. We break down ClaudiaвҖҷs terrifying arrival, LestatвҖҷs manipulative parenting, LouisвҖҷ growing guilt and resistance, and why Episode 4 stands as one of the most disturbing and emotionally rich installments of the season so far. title: Rachel Reilly Talks The Traitors S4 Ep 8
summary: Rachel Reilly Talks The Traitors S4 Ep 8 Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. Rob Cesternino and Pooya welcome special guest Rachel Reilly to the Feedback Show. With two traitors gone for a second consecutive week, the trio breaks down shifting alliances, messy gameplay, and how reality TV relationships reshape the competition. Rachel brings her signature insight from both The Traitors and Big Brother, questioning decisions and celebrating the showвҖҷs evolving style. Conversations center on the standout influence of wardrobe, with Rachel and Rob reflecting on how U.S. contestants bring fashion to the forefront, unlike their international counterparts. Attention shifts to this seasonвҖҷs strategy: the impact of getting out the вҖңgamersвҖқ early, the intense focus on Rob RauschвҖҷs rise as an unexpected traitor, and how his social game keeps the faithfuls on his side. The dynamic between the HousewivesвҖ”especially their reluctance (or not) to vote each other outвҖ”gets a closer look, as Rachel ponders whether this forms a lasting in-game alliance or just fuels more drama. Rachel, Rob, and Pooya dissect the traitor-on-traitor clash between Rob and Candiace, the faith placed in Rob by the other contestants, and the tough choices facing new recruit Eric in the turret. The group also shares casting advice, predictions for the season finale, and behind-the-scenes tales from breakfast drama to the chat BCC groupвҖҷs wild moments.   Traitor-versus-traitor moves outside the turretвҖ”how open conflict shapes suspicion   The evolving role of вҖңgamersвҖқ versus celebs and the fallout of gamer-heavy early boots   Housewives alliance: myth or reality? When loyal friendships blur strategic lines   Casting advice for civilian hopefulsвҖ”why being a sleuth matters more than mission performance   Predictions for endgame: Will Rob cruise to a solo win, or will someone finally catch on to his moves? With fashion statements, new alliances, and questions of loyalty at play, The Traitors Season 4 sets up a tense endgame. Who will spot the clues, and how will friendships and strategy collide as the roundtable heats up? title: Traitors US S4 Week 5 Interview w/ Banished Player
summary: Traitors US S4 Week 5 Interview w/ Banished Player Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. summary: IF THERE ARE ZERO SWEETPEA FANS, THEN WEвҖҷRE DEAD! IвҖҷm joined by my cuzzin Kaya (of the вҖңBravo! WeвҖҷre BlackвҖқ podcast), BUT THIS TIMEвҖҰ itвҖҷs for some Industry chat! As you know, Kaya (and Jay) cover Industry over on BWB as well, and it was time for her to come over for a mid-season check-in. We dived into both episodes 4 and 5 and cackled the entire time. title: Sister Wives 5.4 - Polygamist Cults (W. Notes to Self 444)
summary: We're back to season 5, folks, and i'm tagging in some help. I'll be honest, I was planning on taking a break from collaborations for a bit, but then this episode was a struggle for me right away. The Brown teenagers (or a few of them) go to meet with and help Holding out Help, the organization that helps former polygamists and cult members escape and establish their new lives. Well, we all know a lot more about these things than we did when this first aired. So Melanie and Cory of Notes to Self 444 (themselves former members of the Browns' sect the AUB) were kind enough to come on and help me understand what's all going on beneath the surface here. To me it's the most enlightening podcast i've ever done, thanks to them, so I hope you enjoy. Notes to Self on Youtube. Notes to Self on TikTok. Notes to Self on Instagram. title: Conversation Street Episode 718
summary: On our latest podcast, we chat about the episodes of Corrie shown between the 2nd and the 6th February (Episodes #11,787 - 11,789). Thanks to a couple of sporting events on Tuesday and Thursday, this week's helping of Coronation Street was almost as short as Kevin's temper. However, with Wednesday's Webster-focused episode featuring the big reveal to Carl that Debbie is actually his mum, there was still plenty for us to discuss! The pair of us actually had quite different opinions on that episode too, so it was pretty fun debating what did and didn't work for us! Also this week, a rooftop intervention from Abi leaves Kevin with new perspective on life (and Brian with a sore head!), Todd struggles to cope with no food and no money, and Jodie intrigues us with a mysterious box of trinkets... Up next on the podcast, it's The Kabin, where we discuss some streaming stats from ITVX and get a little overexcited at the prospect of an upcoming Audience With Helen Worth event, then we finish things off with more of your listener feedback. Street Talk - 00:12:09 The Kabin - 01:56:42 Feedback - 02:13:31

title: Traitors US S4 Ep 8 Recap
summary: Traitors US S4 Ep 8 Recap Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. Today, Rob Cesternino and Pooya discuss The Traitors US S4 Episode 8. Rob Cesternino and Pooya recap Episode 8 of The Traitors, analyzing the fallout from ColtonвҖҷs murder and CandaceвҖҷs banishment in a pivotal turning point for the season. They break down how RobвҖҷs decision to allow Candace to target Colton ultimately became the key factor in her downfall, framing it as a calculated sacrifice that shifted suspicion entirely in his favor. The Rob and Pooya focus on the tense confrontation between Rob and Candace in the library, calling it one of the seasonвҖҷs defining strategic moments. They explain how Candace realized she had fallen into a trap, but lacked the numbers and credibility to recover at the round table. Her вҖңthrowaway vote,вҖқ combined with ColtonвҖҷs murder, made it difficult for her to build a convincing case against Rob. Rob and Pooya examine why Candace failed to gain support from key allies and debate whether targeting Stephen instead of Rob could have altered her fate. They also analyze NatalieвҖҷs late vote against Tara, questioning whether it reflected personal frustration or genuine suspicion. Attention then turns to RobвҖҷs continued dominance in the game, including his strong social positioning, ability to maintain trust, and success in avoiding backlash for eliminating close allies. The hosts discuss how ColtonвҖҷs death further protected Rob by reinforcing his image as a loyal player. The episode closes with a breakdown of RobвҖҷs decision to recruit Eric as a new traitor, evaluating the risks and benefits of bringing in a low-suspicion player. With shifting power dynamics and fewer shields left, Rob and Pooya assess how this move could shape the endgame. title: How to Lose Friends and Manifest Legal Trouble: Amanda Frances
summary: Legal Team, weвҖҷre back in Beverly Hills with more research into Amanda Frances. From questionable cease-and-desist letters allegedly sent to Redditors leaving reviews, to a failed federal lawsuit over the вҖңlook and feelвҖқ of another womanвҖҷs finance book. In this episode, we break down the consumer rights issues, the limits of non-disparagement clauses for people leaving honest reviews, and why AmandaвҖҷs attempt to claim ownership over вҖңAs F**kвҖқ and bathtub-money vibes didnвҖҷt exactly impress a federal judge. title: Will the Traitors Go to War?- Traitors S4 Ep 7 Feedback Show
summary: Will the Traitors Go to War?- Traitors S4 Ep 7 Feedback Show Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors US is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. Rob Cesternino and Pooya dig into The Traitors US Season 4, Episode 7, as the aftermath of the Black Banquet pushes the conflict between Rob and Candiace into the spotlight. With Rob and Candiace now sharing the turret and facing a major decision, the discussion zeroes in on crumbling alliances, split strategies, and the uncertain futures of both traders and faithfuls. This episode breaks down the lingering tension from the Black Banquet and traces the main debates emerging among players and fans: Was RobвҖҷs aggressive case against Lisa justified, or did Candiace's offbeat vote reveal more about her own agenda? Rob and Pooya question whether these traders can repair their fractured alliance or are better off cutting ties and bringing someone new into the fold. International adaptations of the show come up as they weigh the effectiveness of recruitment strategy and the dangers of using new traitors as scapegoats. Highlights from Rob and PooyaвҖҷs analysis include: Weighing the merits and pitfalls of recruiting a traitor for short-term gain versus building loyalty for the late game Dissecting Candiace's explanation for her Rob vote, as shared in her recent TV appearance, and what it signals for her approach Assessing the shifting allegiances among the remaining Faithfuls, including how players like Mark, Johnny, and Tara stand out amidst the chaos Debating the best path forward for Rob and Candiace: fix the partnership for mutual benefit, or let the looming civil war play out Rethinking the banquet's twists and how a drawn-out poisoning reveal could have reshaped the trajectory of suspicion With alliances changing and trust unraveling, Rob and Pooya spotlight the critical decisions waiting in Episode 8. title: S2E59-ReRun-REWATCH10-PT03-Cold Harbor
summary: IT'S THE END OF THE SEASON, REFINER!! (Part 3 of 3) Welcome to Part Three of "Cold Harbor", the Season Two Finale of "Severance." This Finale is a HUGE episode with fully half an hour more content than a regular S2 episode. That means we're taking a full THREE episodes of the podcast to cover it. This is Part 3 of 3. This one is CRAZY! We're going to discover there's a hidden goat sacrifice chamber on the Severed Floor. Milchick will get in touch with his inner 'Avenger'. Dylan will show us how it's done when something gets stuck in the vending machine. Oh, and Mark is going to commit a heinous and violent murder but he won't know it. Mark is also going to make a monumental decision at the end of this one. I'm sure you know what it is, but it's still a tearjerker!! Strap in, Refiner...it's time to complete the file called "Cold Harbor.' *** A BIG 'thank you' to Research Volunteer/Producer Refiner Vinny P. Vinny has been providing outstanding research and information during the Season Two Rewatch Episodes. Vinny is also tracking down Interview Victims...er, Subjects! Huge thanks to Adam Scott, star of 'Severance' and host of the Severance Podcast for recording a custom intro for "Severed." Make sure to check out 'The Severance Podcast w/Ben Stiller & Adam Scott" wherever you found this one! A big 'thank you' to friend of the pod Kier Eagan, er I mean Marc Geller! Marc both sat for an interview (make sure to check it out) AND recorded some great bumpers as Kier himself. Follow Marc on Instagram @geller_marc. BIG Thanks to Friend of the Podcast Hayley Erickson-Goelzer. She is Sister to creator Dan Erickson and also a listener. It took getting approval from Dan's Press Person, but thanks to her follow-through we've got a custom bumper from Hayley Erickson-Goelzer!! Yes, that is really and truly Primary Director and Executive Producer Ben Stiller doing a custom bumper for 'Severed.' He actually did this bumper for the show while we were recording an interview for the Official Ben & Adam Podcast. Thank you, Ben for the Bumper and for bringing this incredible show to the screen!! Support the Severed Podcast on Patreon! (Click here) APPLE PODCAST LISTENERS: If you are enjoying "Severed: The Ultimate 'Severance' Podcast" please make sure to leave a 5-star rating (and, if you want, a review telling others to give it a try). Higher rated podcasts get better placement in suggestion lists. It helps more "Severance" fans find the show. Thanks!!! (Unfortunately, I can't respond to any questions or comments made in Apple Podcast Reviews. Send those to: SeveredPod@gmail.com) Season 2 of "Severance" kicked off 1/17/2025 and ran through 3/20/2025.В  The Second Season of the "Severed" Podcast Rewatch Episodes kicked off on April 24th, 2025. To support the Severed Podcast: (www.patreon.com/SeveredPod)В  Join the fun on our Facebook page @SeveredPod. I always try to keep page followersВ  updated on news about the show.  Also, let's talk!! Comments? Theories? Corrections? I LOVE 'EM!! Send to: SeveredPod@gmail.com PLEASE MAKE SURE TO SHARE THE PODCAST WITH YOUR FRIENDS WHO ARE 'SEVERANCE' FANS. THE SHOW GROWS THROUGH WORD OF MOUTH!! Needing your own copies of the Lexington Letter and Orientation Booklet? I've got you covered with downloadable PDFs of both documents: LETTER: LEXINGTONLETTER-TheLetter.pdf  HANDBOOK: LEXINGTONLETTER-MDROrientationHandbook.pdf You haven't completely watched 'Severance' until you've listened to 'Severed'.

title: Sister Wives 20.19 - One on One Part IV

title: Rob's Traitors UK S4 Review
summary: Rob's Traitors UK S4 Review Welcome to RHAP's coverage of ALL the Traitors iterations from around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and Canada. The Traitors is a high-stakes reality competition set in a secluded castle, where contestants must work together to complete challenges and earn a prize potвҖ”while rooting out the secret traitors hidden among them. Today, Rob Cesternino joins Pooya and Annabell to talk through his thoughts on Traitors UK S4. Rob Cesternino joins Pooya and Annabel to break down The Traitors UK Season 4 in a full-spoiler discussion focused on gameplay, strategy, and the seasonвҖҷs defining relationships. Drawing from the finale recap and post-season analysis, the hosts examine how Rachel and Steven navigated constant suspicion to secure a dominant traitor victory. The talk centers on whether Rachel or Steven deserves primary credit for the win. Rob argues that RachelвҖҷs ability to survive repeated public accusations, manage multiple relationships, and redirect suspicion made her the seasonвҖҷs standout strategist, while StevenвҖҷs social capital and calming influence provided essential support. Together, they discuss how the вҖңcontrol freak and quiet oneвҖқ pairing created a highly effective endgame alliance. The panel revisit key faithful players, including Jessie and Harriet, highlighting why strong reads failed to translate into lasting momentum. They also analyze major twists, including the dagger mechanic, secret traitor format, and tie-breaking shield challenge, evaluating how each shaped voting behavior and endgame outcomes. Additional discussion focuses on breakfast strategy, fashion and social distraction, family relationship twists, and whether these elements help or hinder faithful gameplay. Rob, Pooya, and Annabell compare the UK and US versions, arguing that the UK format prioritizes cleaner strategy while the US emphasizes spectacle. The episode closes with broader reflections on future twists, faithful power balance, and what Season 4 reveals about long-term traitor success. With detailed breakdowns of voting patterns, alliance management, and social manipulation, this recap frames the season as one of the franchiseвҖҷs most strategically impressive wins. So, grab your cloak and join the roundtable вҖ” because when it comes to The Traitors, nobody does betrayal breakdowns better than We Know the Traitors. summary: DIVING INTO THE WORLD OF TYLER PERRY!! Written by and starring Tyler Perry (MadeaвҖҷs Family Reunion, Acrimony), the film blends melodrama, faith, romance, and sharp comedy while tackling themes of betrayal, healing, and self-worth. The story follows Helen McCarter (Kimberly Elise вҖ“ Beloved, Set It Off), whose seemingly perfect life collapses when her powerful attorney husband Charles McCarter (Steve Harris вҖ“ The Practice, Minority Report) abruptly abandons her for another woman. Forced to rebuild from the ground up, Helen finds refuge with her outspoken grandmother Madea and begins a slow journey toward independence and emotional recovery. Along the way, she forms a genuine connection with Orlando (Shemar Moore вҖ“ Criminal Minds, S.W.A.T.), whose kindness and patience contrast sharply with the abuse sheвҖҷs endured. Packed with memorable momentsвҖ”from MadeaвҖҷs courtroom antics and brutally honest life advice to the emotional reckoning that tests forgiveness and accountabilityвҖ”Diary of a Mad Black Woman remains one of Tyler PerryвҖҷs most discussed films. We break down its cultural impact, performances, tonal shifts between comedy and drama, and why this film still resonates with audiences nearly two decades later. title: Conversation Street Episode 717
summary: On our latest podcast, we take a look at the episodes of Coronation Street shown between the 26th and the 30th January 2026 (Episodes #11,782 - 11,786). Definitely a bit of a divisive week, this one, with opinions all over the place online about Billy's funeral and Bernie's subsequent getting off her cake, as she put it. Even the pair of us felt quite differently to each other about how Wednesday's episode went down, with Gemma praising the writing and direction whilst Michael didn't think it quite did enough to honour Billy's time in the show amongst the Carl vs Theo drama. Speaking of which, we chat for quite a bit this week about who we'd prefer to ultimately see come on top there, and how much Theo is actually in control of what he's doing. One thing we both agreed on, though - which seemingly a В sizeable chunk of Corrie fans online disagreed with - was that Thursday's Bernie-centric saga was fantastic, plus we were both very chuffed to see Sam back on centre stage on Monday's episode - and think that Megan will prove to be quite a force for him to reckon with! Another fairly news-lite week saw us skipping The Kabin again, so after Street Talk, we head straight to the feedback section for more of your fabulous emails. title: Incel In A Cell
podcast_name: Albert Square: After Dark - An EastEnders Podcast
summary: This week, the trial of Joel Marshall arrives and it's a couple of drama filled days in court - but can the troubled youth ever really change?  Meanwhile, Bea has her eyes set on the minute mart and she's prepared to do what it takes to get a job there - but does she have her eyes on Honey too?  Elsewhere, Vicki and Zack let passion get the better of them and Elaine and Ian's feud escalates, with Kathy in the crossfire.

title: DAREDEVIL: BORN AGAIN Season 2 Trailer Reaction, WONDER MAN Failed Marketing, & NEW Black Panther?!
summary: DARREN ARONOFSKY MADE AN A.I. SERIES?! WHAT?!  Greg Alba & Coy Jandreau break down the latest Marvel news and controversies, starting with our reaction to the Daredevil: Born Again Season 2 trailer and what it signals for the future of street-level Marvel storytelling.  We also tackle the growing conversation around Wonder Man, a series we believe is genuinely strong, but one thatвҖҷs been hurt by confusing and underwhelming marketing, especially surrounding the Marvel Television and Marvel Spotlight branding. We discuss how mixed messaging, unclear positioning, and low visibility may be sabotaging audience awareness despite solid creative work. On top of that, scoopers are reporting that Damson Idris has allegedly had a meeting with Marvel Studios, sparking speculation about a potential new Black Panther direction and what that could mean for WakandaвҖҷs future in the MCU. We break down the rumor, why fans are split, and what Marvel may be looking for moving forward. Finally, we dig into the controversy surrounding the Darren Aronofsky AI-driven series, unpacking the creative, ethical, and industry implications of using artificial intelligence in storytelling вҖ” and why this debate matters right now more than ever.
